perturbation.original_text: Pretzel Time may charge fees for refresher training courses for previously trained and experienced managers. Fees for special programs will be based upon Pretzel Time's actual costs and attendance shall be required. Attendance at retraining programs or seminars shall be at Franchisee's sole expense, provided, however, that attendance will not be required at more than two (2) such programs in any calendar year and shall not collectively exceed ten (10) business days in duration during any calendar year.
perturbation.changed_text: Pretzel Time may charge fees for refresher training courses for previously trained and experienced managers. Fees for special programs will be based upon Pretzel Time's actual costs and attendance shall be required. Attendance at retraining programs or seminars shall be at Franchisee's expense, provided, however, that attendance will be required at least four (4) times in any calendar year and may exceed twenty (20) business days in duration during any calendar year, at the sole discretion of Pretzel Time.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by changing the mandatory training frequency and duration. Originally, attendance was capped at two programs/10 days per year; now, it's a minimum of four programs and potentially over 20 days, at Pretzel Time's discretion. This directly contradicts the previous limitation, creating uncertainty about the franchisee's training obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pretzel Time shall provide re-training programs at a location of Pretzel Time's choice from time to time for experienced franchisees and their managers and/or employees. Pretzel Time may charge fees for refresher training courses for previously trained and experienced managers. Fees for special programs will be based upon Pretzel Time's actual costs and attendance shall be required. Attendance at retraining programs or seminars shall be at Franchisee's expense, provided, however, that attendance will be required at least four (4) times in any calendar year and may exceed twenty (20) business days in duration during any calendar year, at the sole discretion of Pretzel Time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text limited attendance at retraining programs to a maximum of two programs and ten business days per year. The changed text mandates a minimum of four programs and potentially exceeding twenty business days, which directly conflicts with the previous limitation. The contradicted text is in the same location as the changed text, but the change itself causes the contradiction. This inconsistency regarding the frequency and duration of mandatory training creates a clear and impactful conflict within the contract, satisfying the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, Franchisee shall identify himself as the independent owner of the Unit in the manner prescribed by Pretzel Time. Franchisee agrees to give such notices of trademark and service mark registrations as Pretzel Time may specify and to obtain such business name registrations as may be required under applicable law. Franchisee shall not at any time during the term of this Agreement or after its termination, contest the validity or ownership of any of the Marks or assist any other person in contesting the validity or ownership of the Marks.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, Franchisee shall identify himself as an extension of Pretzel Time and its brand image, foregoing any explicit declaration of independent ownership unless directed otherwise. Franchisee agrees to give such notices of trademark and service mark registrations as Pretzel Time may specify and to obtain such business name registrations as may be required under applicable law. Franchisee shall not at any time during the term of this Agreement or after its termination, contest the validity or ownership of any of the Marks or assist any other person in contesting the validity or ownership of the Marks.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by altering how the franchisee identifies themselves. Originally, the franchisee identifies as an 'independent owner,' now it changes to identifies himself as an 'extension of Pretzel Time' This introduces ambiguity regarding the franchisee's operational autonomy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is understood and agreed by the parties hereto that Franchisee is an independent contractor and is not an agent, partner, joint venturer, or employee of Pretzel Time. Pretzel Time and Franchisee agree that nothing in this Agreement is intended to create a fiduciary relationship between them. Franchisee shall have no right to bind or obligate Pretzel Time in any way nor shall he represent that he has any right to do so. Pretzel Time shall have no control over the terms and conditions of employment of Franchisee's employees. In all public records and in his relationship with other persons, on stationery, business forms and checks, Franchisee shall indicate his independent ownership of the franchised Unit and that he is a franchisee of Pretzel Time. Franchisee shall exhibit on the premises in such places as may be designated by Pretzel Time, a Pretzel Time approved notice that the franchised Unit is operated by an independent operator and not by Pretzel Time or Pretzel Time's Affiliates, which operate company owned franchises. Franchisee shall take all legal steps such as a fictitious name registration to ensure Franchisee's independent business status.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text'. The original text states the franchisee identifies as an 'independent owner,' aligning with the section stating that the franchisee is an 'independent contractor.' The changed text, however, states the franchisee identifies as an 'extension of Pretzel Time,' which is in direct conflict with the 'independent contractor' status as stated in the contradicted text. This creates a strong in-text contradiction regarding the franchisee's operational autonomy and their relationship with Pretzel Time. The 'Contradicted Text' emphasizes the franchisee's independent ownership in all public records and relationships, whereas the 'Changed Text' suggests the opposite, causing a clear and impactful contradiction.
